title: 007-root claims bitemporal wiring is still pending, but 011 follow-up says it was done
evidence: Root (line 62): 'The bitemporal edge writer must be wired into the `replaceEdges` reindex path behind its read flag before that flag can flip. The validation and review phases both recorded the wiring as the one honest remaining step.' vs 011 follow-up (line 22): 'wired the bitemporal close-and-insert writer through the full reindex path' and (line 37): '`replaceEdges` and `pruneDanglingEdges` in `code-graph-db.ts` changed to close-not-delete under the bitemporal flag.'
fix: Update 007-root.md line 62 to state the wiring was completed in phase 011, removing it from the open follow-ups or reclassifying it as a completed item.

title: 010 validation says bitemporal wiring unconfirmed, but 011 says it was done
evidence: 010 (line 62): 'Bitemporal reads consumer wiring is not confirmed end-to-end. The review recommends re-classifying bitemporal reads as REFINE before final production graduation decision.' vs 011 (line 22): 'wired the bitemporal close-and-insert writer through the full reindex path and set the degree-cap default to 15' and (lines 48-49): fixes for generation bump and lineage valid_at.
fix: Update 010's follow-ups section to note that the wiring gap was resolved by phase 011, so the REFINE recommendation is superseded.

title: JSDoc says default OFF, code returns default ON for RELEVANCE_AWARE_GAP
evidence: JSDoc (lines 1030-1033): 'Default: FALSE (opt-in): with the flag OFF detectEvidenceGap returns the exact Z-score result unchanged, so flag-off is byte-identical. Set SPECKIT_RELEVANCE_AWARE_GAP=true to enable.' Code (lines 1036-1039): returns `true` unless env is explicitly 'false'/'0'/'off'. ENV_REFERENCE.md line 243 also says 'Default OFF'.
fix: Update the JSDoc at lines 1030-1033 to say 'Default: ON (graduated)' and update ENV_REFERENCE.md line 243 default column from `true` description to clarify graduated-ON semantics, matching the actual code behavior.

title: Flag-ceiling test lists RELEVANCE_AWARE_GAP as opt-in unceilinged but code defaults it ON
evidence: Line 281: `'SPECKIT_RELEVANCE_AWARE_GAP'` listed in ACKNOWLEDGED_UNCEILINGED_FLAGS with surrounding comment (lines 273-275): 'Lexical-floor and scoring-hardening opt-in flags read in search-flags.ts. They reshape relevance scoring rather than join the boolean ceiling soak.' But the actual code (search-flags.ts:1036-1039) defaults this flag to ON, making it a graduated flag, not opt-in.
fix: Move SPECKIT_RELEVANCE_AWARE_GAP from ACKNOWLEDGED_UNCEILINGED_FLAGS to ALL_SPECKIT_FLAGS (the ceiling soak list) since it is now graduated-ON, or add an explicit comment that it is graduated but excluded from the soak for domain reasons.
